source_pr.title: executor, planner: `InitLogger` before `CreateMockStore` in tests to avoid data race
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?


Issue Number: close #39892

Problem Summary:

Please see the issue.

### What is changed and how it works?

Please see the issue.

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

symptoms: Go race detector reports a data race in slow-log / binary-plan tests caused by global logger initialization racing with background goroutines started by `CreateMockStore`.
failure_signature: DATA RACE around `logutil.InitLogger(...)` and background logging from mock-store/domain goroutines.
root_cause_explanation: Several tests re-initialize global logging/config via `logutil.InitLogger(...)` but previously created the mock store/testkit first. `CreateMockStore(...)` can start background goroutines that log, which can race with the global logger init and trigger `-race` failures. The fix moves mock-store creation to happen after logger initialization (and adjusts cleanup), so background goroutines start only after the global logger is configured.
fix_pattern: Initialize global logger/config first, then create the mock store/testkit so background goroutines cannot race with `logutil.InitLogger(...)`.

evidence: evidence: executor, planner: `InitLogger` before `CreateMockStore` in tests to avoid data race
